RETURN c.name AS name

cases.question: Count the number of articles for each sentiment bucket: positive (> 0.3), neutral (-0.3 to 0.3), and negative (< -0.3). Return bucket name and count.
cases.generated_cypher:
CYPHER 25
MATCH (a:Article)
WITH CASE
  WHEN a.sentiment > 0.3 THEN 'positive'
  WHEN a.sentiment < -0.3 THEN 'negative'
  ELSE 'neutral'
END AS bucket
RETURN bucket, count(*) AS count
ORDER BY count DESC

CYPHER 25

WITH org, count(a) AS mentionCount

}

cases.question: For each top-level organization (not a subsidiary, tested with NOT EXISTS), use a CALL subquery to compute both the count of direct subsidiaries and the average sentiment of all articles mentioning it. Only return organizations that have at least one article. Order by average sentiment ascending, limit to 10.
cases.generated_cypher:
CYPHER 25
MATCH (org:Organization)
WHERE NOT EXISTS { (org)<-[:HAS_SUBSIDIARY]-() }
CALL (org) {
  OPTIONAL MATCH (org)<-[:HAS_SUBSIDIARY]-(sub:Organization)
  WITH org, count(sub) AS subsidiaryCount
  OPTIONAL MATCH (org)<-[:MENTIONS]-(a:Article)
  RETURN subsidiaryCount, count(a) AS articleCount, avg(a.sentiment) AS avgSentiment
}
WITH org, subsidiaryCount, articleCount, avgSentiment
WHERE articleCount > 0
RETURN org.name AS organization, subsidiaryCount, articleCount, round(avgSentiment, 4) AS avgSentiment
ORDER BY avgSentiment ASC
LIMIT 10

gate_details.reason: No deprecated syntax or operators detected

cases.question: Find organizations that appear together in articles with both positive sentiment (>0.3) AND negative sentiment (<-0.3) — i.e., the same two organizations are co-mentioned in at least one positive article and at least one negative article. Return the organization pair and counts for each sentiment type. Limit to 10 pairs.
cases.generated_cypher:
CYPHER 25
MATCH (o1:Organization)<-[:MENTIONS]-(a:Article)-[:MENTIONS]->(o2:Organization)
WHERE o1.name < o2.name
WITH o1, o2,
     COUNT { MATCH (o1)<-[:MENTIONS]-(pos:Article)-[:MENTIONS]->(o2) WHERE pos.sentiment > 0.3 } AS positiveCount,
     COUNT { MATCH (o1)<-[:MENTIONS]-(neg:Article)-[:MENTIONS]->(o2) WHERE neg.sentiment < -0.3 } AS negativeCount
WHERE positiveCount > 0 AND negativeCount > 0
RETURN o1.name AS org1, o2.name AS org2, positiveCount, negativeCount
ORDER BY positiveCount + negativeCount DESC
LIMIT 10

cases.question: Build a multi-level aggregation query: first use CALL subqueries to compute per-organization metrics (article count, avg sentiment, subsidiary count), then aggregate at the category level (via HAS_CATEGORY) to return: category name, number of orgs, total articles, avg sentiment, and total subsidiaries. Limit to 10 categories.
cases.generated_cypher:
CYPHER 25
MATCH (org:Organization)
WHERE NOT EXISTS { (org)<-[:HAS_SUBSIDIARY]-() }
CALL (org) {
  MATCH (org)<-[:MENTIONS]-(a:Article)
  RETURN count(a) AS articleCount, avg(a.sentiment) AS avgSentiment
}
CALL (org) {
  MATCH (org)-[:HAS_SUBSIDIARY]->(sub:Organization)
  RETURN count(sub) AS subsidiaryCount
}
MATCH (cat:Category)-[:HAS_CATEGORY]->(org)
WITH cat,
     count(org)            AS orgCount,
     sum(articleCount)     AS totalArticles,
     avg(avgSentiment)     AS catAvgSentiment,
     sum(subsidiaryCount)  AS totalSubsidiaries
RETURN cat.name           AS category,
       orgCount,
       totalArticles,
       catAvgSentiment,
       totalSubsidiaries
ORDER BY totalArticles DESC
LIMIT 10

gate_details.reason: No deprecated syntax or operators detected

cases.question: For each organization with at least 5 subsidiaries, use a CALL subquery with an OPTIONAL MATCH inside to find the best article (highest sentiment) mentioning that organization, and use a second CALL subquery with a quantified path expression {2,2} to find organizations exactly 2 HAS_SUBSIDIARY hops away. Return org name, best article title (null if none), and list of second-level subsidiary names. Limit to 10.
cases.generated_cypher:
CYPHER 25
MATCH (org:Organization)
WHERE COUNT { (org)-[:HAS_SUBSIDIARY]->() } >= 5
CALL (org) {
  OPTIONAL MATCH (org)<-[:MENTIONS]-(a:Article)
  RETURN a.title AS bestArticleTitle
  ORDER BY a.sentiment DESC
  LIMIT 1
}
CALL (org) {
  MATCH (org)-[:HAS_SUBSIDIARY]->{2,2}(sub:Organization)
  RETURN collect(DISTINCT sub.name) AS secondLevelSubsidiaries
}
RETURN org.name AS orgName, bestArticleTitle, secondLevelSubsidiaries
LIMIT 10

cases.question: Write a query that uses EXISTS subquery expressions to classify organizations into four groups: (1) has subsidiaries AND has articles, (2) has subsidiaries but NO articles, (3) no subsidiaries but HAS articles, (4) neither. Use CASE WHEN with EXISTS{} to assign the group. Return each group label and the count of organizations in it.
cases.generated_cypher:
CYPHER 25
MATCH (o:Organization)
WITH o,
  CASE
    WHEN EXISTS { (o)-[:HAS_SUBSIDIARY]->() } AND EXISTS { ()-[:ABOUT]->(o) } THEN 'has subsidiaries AND has articles'
    WHEN EXISTS { (o)-[:HAS_SUBSIDIARY]->() } AND NOT EXISTS { ()-[:ABOUT]->(o) } THEN 'has subsidiaries but NO articles'
    WHEN NOT EXISTS { (o)-[:HAS_SUBSIDIARY]->() } AND EXISTS { ()-[:ABOUT]->(o) } THEN 'no subsidiaries but HAS articles'
    ELSE 'neither'
  END AS groupLabel
RETURN groupLabel, count(o) AS organizationCount
ORDER BY organizationCount DESC

gate_details.reason: No deprecated syntax or operators detected
